evidence: Top-level packages are purpose-named and reasonably scoped: grammar, indexer, query, tagging, api, config, tools, utils, corpus — eight packages for ~56 production files is appropriate fan-out.

indexer/hybrid/ contains 22 files flat (threshold: 10), mixing at least five distinct concern groups: core processing (HybridIndexer, CollocationsBuilder, SinglePassConlluProcessor, HybridConllUProcessor), data model records (Collocation, CollocationEntry, TermStatistics, SentenceDocument), codec/IO layer (LemmaIdsCodec, LemmaLexiconReader, LemmaLexiconWriter, PairRunIO, TokenSequenceCodec, IntArrayBuffer), statistics builders (StatisticsIndexBuilder, StatisticsReader, StatisticsConverter), and migration/utility helpers (LexiconFromStatsMigrator, LongIntHashMap, LongIntPairSort, LemmaIdAssigner, PositionalTokenStream). A developer looking for, say, 'where is the binary serialization?' must scan 22 files with no sub-package guidance.

query/ contains 15 files mixing predicate combinators (AndPredicate, OrPredicate, NotPredicate, LemmaPredicate, TagPredicate, PosGroupPredicate, WordPredicate — 7 small classes), infrastructure interfaces/models (QueryExecutor, QueryResults, Token, TokenWindow, TokenPredicate), executor implementations (BlackLabQueryExecutor, CQLVerifier), and the high-level semantic exploration component (SemanticFieldExplorer at 889 LOC). These concern layers have different change rates and ownership boundaries.

TestQuery.java lives at the repository root with package declaration absent, fan_in=0, fan_out=0. It contains a hardcoded Windows absolute path ('D:\\corpora_philsci\\bi') and is a scratch development driver that was not promoted into tools/ or removed. It imports from query.BlackLabQueryExecutor.

corpus/ has 2 files (CorpusBuilder, PostgresCorpusSampler) with avg_fan_in=0.0 and avg_fan_out=0.0 — nothing imports from this package and it has no test coverage. It is functionally orphaned and creates a misleading directory entry that implies corpus-building is a supported path.

strengths: Top-level package taxonomy is well-named and reflects real system concerns: grammar (CQL parsing), indexer (corpus ingestion), query (search execution), tagging (NLP pipeline), api (HTTP layer), config (grammar configuration), tools (CLI diagnostics), utils (scoring math).

The indexer sub-package split into indexer/ (LuceneIndexer), indexer/blacklab/ (BlackLabConllUIndexer), and indexer/hybrid/ (hybrid pipeline) correctly isolates three distinct indexing strategies as sibling namespaces, making strategy substitution navigable.
Small utility packages (tools/ with 2 files, utils/ implied, config/ with 1 file) are appropriately thin and do not try to accumulate unrelated code.
package_organization.issue_character: The issues are structural overloading and a placement straggler: two directories exceed the 10-file threshold (indexer/hybrid/ at 22, query/ at 15) with clearly mixed sub-concerns that lack internal sub-package organization, and one root-level straggler file was never promoted out of scratch status.
package_organization.score_rationale: The codebase sits below the 'solid but uneven' (80) band because the two largest directories — which contain the bulk of the production logic — require flat scanning to navigate rather than sub-package guidance. indexer/hybrid/ at 22 files mixing codec, model, statistics, and processing is the most significant drag: a developer adding a new collocation serialization format has no clear sub-folder boundary to work within. query/ at 15 files conflating predicate primitives with executor implementations and semantic analytics is a secondary but real boundary problem. The top-level taxonomy is genuinely well-structured and earns the score above the 'significant drag' floor (60), but the two overloaded flat directories prevent a higher rating.

issues.summary: indexer/hybrid/ (22 files) mixes 5 distinct concern groups with no sub-package separation
evidence: 22 Java files in a single flat directory, exceeding the >10 mixed-concern threshold.

Five distinct concern groups identified: (1) processing pipeline — HybridIndexer, HybridConllUProcessor, SinglePassConlluProcessor, CollocationsBuilder; (2) domain model records — Collocation, CollocationEntry, TermStatistics, SentenceDocument; (3) codec/binary-IO — LemmaIdsCodec, LemmaLexiconReader, LemmaLexiconWriter, PairRunIO, TokenSequenceCodec, IntArrayBuffer; (4) statistics — StatisticsIndexBuilder, StatisticsReader, StatisticsConverter; (5) utilities/migration — LexiconFromStatsMigrator, LongIntHashMap, LongIntPairSort, LemmaIdAssigner, PositionalTokenStream.
CollocationsBuilder alone is 1139 LOC sitting adjacent to tiny data-carrier records (Collocation at ~40 LOC), making importance and ownership opaque.
issues.suggestion: Stage a reorg in three passes: (1) Create indexer/hybrid/model/ and move Collocation.java, CollocationEntry.java, TermStatistics.java, SentenceDocument.java — update package declarations and all imports (only internal to indexer/hybrid and CollocationsBuilder need updating). Validate: mvn compile. (2) Create indexer/hybrid/codec/ and move LemmaIdsCodec.java, LemmaLexiconReader.java, LemmaLexiconWriter.java, PairRunIO.java, TokenSequenceCodec.java, IntArrayBuffer.java. Validate: mvn compile. (3) Create indexer/hybrid/stats/ and move StatisticsIndexBuilder.java, StatisticsReader.java, StatisticsConverter.java; move LongIntHashMap.java, LongIntPairSort.java, LexiconFromStatsMigrator.java, LemmaIdAssigner.java, PositionalTokenStream.java to indexer/hybrid/util/. Validate: mvn test. The top-level processing classes (HybridIndexer, CollocationsBuilder, HybridConllUProcessor, SinglePassConlluProcessor) remain directly under indexer/hybrid/ as they are the primary entry points.

issues.summary: query/ (15 files) conflates predicate primitives, domain model, executor implementations, and semantic analytics
evidence: Seven predicate combinator classes (AndPredicate, OrPredicate, NotPredicate, LemmaPredicate, TagPredicate, PosGroupPredicate, WordPredicate) plus the TokenPredicate interface are micro-classes (each <30 LOC) that form a self-contained predicate algebra, yet live alongside 887-LOC BlackLabQueryExecutor and 889-LOC SemanticFieldExplorer.
SemanticFieldExplorer implements a bootstrapped semantic-class discovery algorithm — a different concern layer (analytics/exploration) from the raw query execution infrastructure in the same package.
Token and TokenWindow are domain model objects used across predicates and verifiers; they have no ownership signal differentiating them from executor-level utilities.
issues.suggestion: Two-step reorg: (1) Create query/predicate/ and move AndPredicate.java, OrPredicate.java, NotPredicate.java, LemmaPredicate.java, TagPredicate.java, PosGroupPredicate.java, WordPredicate.java, TokenPredicate.java, Token.java, TokenWindow.java — update package declarations and imports in CQLVerifier and any class that constructs predicates. Validate: mvn compile. (2) Move SemanticFieldExplorer.java to a new top-level package query/exploration/ (or promote it to a sibling of query/ as exploration/) since it operates at the application feature level, not the query primitive level. Validate: mvn test.

issues.summary: TestQuery.java at repository root: packageless scratch file with hardcoded Windows path
evidence: TestQuery.java has no package declaration, fan_in=0, fan_out=0 (blind packet root_files entry confirms peripheral role).
Contains hardcoded Windows absolute path: String indexPath = "D:\\corpora_philsci\\bi"; — a development artifact, not a deployable utility.
Imports pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.query.BlackLabQueryExecutor but lives outside the Maven source tree (src/main/java/...), making it uncompilable by standard mvn build.
issues.suggestion: Either delete TestQuery.java (preferred — it is a personal scratch file with no general utility) or migrate it into tools/ as a proper CLI diagnostic: (1) Move to src/main/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/tools/TestQuery.java, (2) add package declaration pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.tools, (3) replace hardcoded path with a CLI argument (args[0]), (4) wire it into Main.java's command dispatch. Validate: mvn compile && git rm TestQuery.java from root if deleted.

issues.summary: corpus/ package (2 files, avg_fan_in=0) is functionally orphaned and misleads navigation
evidence: directory_profiles shows avg_fan_in=0.0 and avg_fan_out=0.0 for corpus/ — nothing in the codebase imports from this package.
CorpusBuilder imports from indexer.LuceneIndexer and tagging.PosTagger, meaning it depends on the main production path but is not itself on any production path (not wired into Main.java).
PostgresCorpusSampler references PostgreSQL JDBC which is not in pom.xml — this is dead experimental code masquerading as a production package.
issues.suggestion: Audit whether corpus/ is intentionally preserved for future use or is dead code. If dead: (1) git rm -r src/main/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/corpus/, (2) verify mvn compile still passes, (3) update README if it references corpus-building from PostgreSQL. If intentionally kept: move to a clearly-named experimental/ or tools/corpus/ sub-package and add a README note marking it as non-production.

root_causes: Incremental growth without partitioning: indexer/hybrid/ and query/ grew organically as features were added, with new files dropped flat into existing directories rather than prompting sub-package creation.
Debug artifacts not cleaned before commit: TestQuery.java and the orphaned corpus/ package are remnants of exploratory development that were never removed or properly promoted.
likely_symptoms: hybrid_flat_overload — the 22-file count is objectively over threshold but individual file quality may be fine
corpus_orphaned_package — the zero fan_in/out is a strong orphan signal but could reflect legitimate future-reserved code
